100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On July 23, 1926, the United States faced a national crisis as a heat wave persisted, with temperatures soaring across the Midwest, prompting health warnings and concerns for the vulnerable. In international news, the League of Nations continued discussions on disarmament, with member nations expressing varying degrees of commitment to reducing military arsenals. Meanwhile, in the realm of arts, the Parisian fashion scene buzzed with the latest summer collections, showcasing bold styles that reflected the Roaring Twenties' spirit.
